pending.body: fix-now medium: Final-review voter and arbiter prompts still tell reviewers that a mapped planned-elsewhere/covered-by amendment will not trigger a restart, but the current implementation deliberately treats every classification as restart-triggering via ReviewFindingClass::triggers_restart() and needs_restart. This can bias reviewers into accepting amendments under false routing semantics. Affected lines: src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2539, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2545, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2586, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2592, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2088, src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:44. Remediation: update the voter/arbiter note to say classification is preserved for downstream routing but currently remains fix-now-equivalent/restart-triggering, or remove the no-restart note until the later routing bead lands.

fix-now medium: Review-stage remediation drops legacy follow_up_or_amendments whenever classified_findings is non-empty. validation_follow_ups() switches entirely to classified_findings at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8196 and maps only that list at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8204, while the review contract still requires follow_up_or_amendments for non-approved outcomes at src/contexts/workflow_composition/contracts.rs:230. A payload containing classified metadata for one item plus additional legacy follow-ups will silently queue only the classified subset, changing existing default behavior and losing requested fixes. Remediation: either validate that classified_findings fully covers follow_up_or_amendments, or merge any unclassified follow_up_or_amendments not represented in classified_findings as FixCurrentBead before building QueuedAmendment records.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
